data.title: Metaplanet Plans a Further $21M Bond Sale to Buy More BTC
data.description:
• Metaplanet said it plans to sell another $21.25 million of bonds to fund further bitcoin purchases.
  • The Tokyo-based firm agreed to issue the debt to EVO FUND on May 9, making it the third such sale in the space of a week.
  • Metaplanet's bitcoin stash currently sits at 5,555 BTC.

data.title: Florida Pharma Firm Will Use XRP for Real-Time Payments in $50M Financing Deal
data.description:
• A Florida pharmaceutical distributor, Wellgistics Health, is integrating XRP as a treasury and payments asset in a $50 million financing deal.
  • The company plans to use XRP for real-time payments and to manage vendor payouts across its pharmacy network.
  • Wellgistics aims to reduce banking delays and settlement costs by using XRP, which settles transactions in 3-5 seconds.

data.title: Bitcoin's Price Surge to $104K Liquidates Nearly $400M in Bearish BTC Bets, Opening Doors to Further Gains
data.description:
• Bitcoin's price surged over 3% to $102,500, triggering $400 million in short position liquidations.
  • The rally followed a U.K. trade deal announcement and record ETF inflows exceeding $40 billion.

data.title: Explosive ETH, ADA, DOGE Moves Spur $800M in Short Liquidations, Highest Since 2023
data.description:
• Ether surged 20%, leading a broad crypto rally and triggering over $750 million in short liquidations, the highest since 2023.
  • Major altcoins like DOGE and Cardano's ADA rose more than 10%, while Solana's SOL, BNB, and XRP increased by at least 7%.
  • The rally was fueled by bullish sentiment and a trade deal between the U.S. and the UK, with Binance and OKX accounting for over $500 million in liquidations.

data.title: ETH Surges 20%, Biggest Gain Since 2021 as Pectra Upgrade Helps Restore 'Confidence'
data.description:
• Ethereum's Pectra upgrade has led to a nearly 20% increase in ETH, trading above $2,100.
  • The upgrade raises the staking limit and improves wallet usability, among other enhancements.
  • Despite the rally, weak network activity suggests a full recovery to previous highs is not imminent.

data.title: Coinbase Earnings, Sales Disappoints Wall Street as Trading Volume Drops 10% Amid Market Turmoil
data.description:
• Coinbase reported $2 billion in Q1 revenue, falling short of analyst expectations and down from $2.27 billion in Q4.
  • Trading volume dropped 10% to $393.1 billion, with transaction revenue declining 19% as market volatility failed to boost activity.
  • Analysts had already cut forecasts ahead of the report, citing weaker trading since January amid macroeconomic uncertainty.

data.title: Coinbase's $2.9B Deribit Deal a 'Legitimate Threat' for Peers, Wall Street Analysts Say
data.description:
• Coinbase’s $2.9 billion acquisition of Deribit vaults it to the top of the global crypto options market, surpassing Binance and others.
  • Analysts say the deal marks the start of a consolidation cycle as Coinbase flexes its public-market advantage to absorb key players.
  • The move expands Coinbase’s international reach and bolsters earnings with Deribit’s steady options revenue, estimated at up to $450M in 2024.

data.title: Meta Is Looking to Enter Red-Hot Stablecoin Market: Fortune
data.description:
• Meta is planning to introduce a stablecoin for managing payouts, according to sources cited by Fortune.
  • The company has hired Ginger Baker, with crypto experience, as vice president of product to aid its stablecoin efforts.
  • Meta's new crypto venture follows the collapse of its previous blockchain project, Diem, amid regulatory challenges.

data.title: Bitcoin $120K Target for 2Q May Be Too Conservative: Standard Chartered
data.description:
• Standard Chartered said its second-quarter bitcoin price target of $120,000 may be too low.
  • Spot bitcoin ETF net inflows were more than $4 billion in the last three weeks, when adjusted for hedge fund basis trades, the bank said.
  • Strategy's bitcoin stash could grow to over 6% of the crypto's total future supply, analyst Geoff Kendrick wrote.

data.title: Bitcoin Tops $100K for First Time in 3 Months; Are Upside Targets Too Low?
data.description:
• Bitcoin is back above $100,000 after dropping just under $75,000 following President Trump's early April tariff announcements.
  • Traditional markets have joined crypto in rallying following the initial panic over the tariff news.
  • Standard Chartered's Geoff Kendrick says flows are the key dynamic in this latest move.
